title: selinum pecten-veneris
intro: Selinum pecten-veneris, also known as the Venetian fern or Lady's crown, is a plant species in the carrot family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Selinum pecten-veneris include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in urinary issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
